eras.name: Mali Empire Golden Age
eras.description: A period of immense wealth where gold and cotton were primary status symbols. Clothing was characterized by voluminous robes and intricate weaving, reflecting the empire's dominance in trans-Saharan trade.
eras.aiImagePrompt: Historical illustration of a 14th-century Malian noble in a voluminous white cotton Grand Boubou with intricate gold embroidery, wearing a large structured turban, set against the mud-brick architecture of Timbuktu.

eras.name: Bamana Cultural Hegemony
eras.description: Focus shifted toward symbolic textiles and tribal identity. This era saw the refinement of Bogolanfini (mud cloth) as a medium for storytelling and spiritual protection.
eras.aiImagePrompt: A 19th-century Bamana hunter wearing a brown cotton shirt adorned with leather amulets, cowrie shells, and mirrors, carrying a traditional bow, photorealistic style.

eras.name: French Soudan Era
eras.description: The introduction of European tailoring and industrial fabrics mixed with local silhouettes. A distinct 'evolue' style emerged among the urban elite in Bamako.
eras.aiImagePrompt: 1940s Bamako street scene, men in a mix of French colonial khaki suits and traditional cotton caps, women in vibrant patterned wax prints with structured headwraps.

eras.name: Post-Independence Modernism
eras.description: A celebration of African identity led by photographers like Malick Sidibé and Seydou Keïta. Youth culture embraced bell-bottoms made from local textiles and sharp, minimalist tailoring.
keyGarments: A-line mini dresses in wax print
eras.aiImagePrompt: Black and white studio portrait style of the 1960s, a young Malian couple posing with a vintage radio; man in flared trousers and a slim-fit shirt, woman in a geometric print shift dress.

eras.name: Modern Malian Renaissance
eras.description: The rise of high-fashion Bazin Riche and the globalization of Bogolan. Malian designers are now integrating traditional hand-dyeing techniques into global haute couture silhouettes.
eras.aiImagePrompt: Modern Malian fashion editorial, a model wearing a structural avant-garde gown made of black and white Bogolanfini, high-contrast lighting, desert background.

textiles.name: Bogolanfini
textiles.description: Handmade Malian cotton fabric dyed with fermented mud and plant extracts.
textiles.technique: Discharge dyeing and hand-painting with mud paste

textiles.name: Bazin Riche
textiles.description: A high-quality damask cotton that is hand-dyed in vibrant colors and beaten with wooden mallets to create a permanent metallic sheen.
textiles.technique: Resist-dyeing and manual pounding for luster

textiles.name: Leppi
textiles.description: A traditional Fulani fabric made of thin strips of indigo-dyed cotton sewn together.

climate.fashionImplication: Extreme heat necessitates loose-fitting, breathable natural fibers like cotton to allow air circulation. The low humidity favors the use of heavy indigo and mud dyes which remain stable in dry heat.

culturalInfluences: The legendary wealth of Mansa Musa

emergingTrends: Digital printing of traditional mud-cloth patterns
